title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today's signal is concentrated in evaluation, auditing, and governance rather than new model capabilities. The strongest updates push toward more verifiable assessments of deletion claims, agent robustness, and content provenance, while standards and policy bodies continue shifting from one-time assurance toward ongoing monitoring and operational accountability.

items.title: Google proposes a framework for auditing machine unlearning
items.why_it_matters: Auditable unlearning is central to privacy, compliance, and verifying whether deletion claims actually hold.

items.title: OpenAI backs EU work on trustworthy AI and content transparency
items.why_it_matters: Provenance and transparency standards affect how AI-generated content is labeled, traced, and governed in Europe.

items.title: NIST argues AI security should move to continuous monitoring and updates
items.why_it_matters: It reinforces that AI assurance cannot be a one-time certification for evolving systems.

items.title: AgentBeats proposes an open, standardized harness for agent evaluation
items.why_it_matters: Standardized agent assessment could improve reproducibility and comparability across safety and capability evaluations.

items.title: EvoArena benchmarks how agent memory changes in dynamic environments
items.why_it_matters: Dynamic-environment testing targets a real deployment risk: agents drifting as conditions and information change.

items.title: One Polluted Page Is Enough examines web-content pollution in generative recommenders
items.why_it_matters: It highlights a concrete integrity risk for retrieval-augmented systems exposed to manipulated web content.

items.title: EpiBench introduces verifiable evaluation for AI agents in epigenomics analysis
items.why_it_matters: Verifiable, deterministic grading is useful for trustworthy evaluation in high-stakes scientific agent settings.

items.title: Beyond the IT Checklist argues for a cyber safety standard of care
items.why_it_matters: It reframes cyber governance around safety outcomes, not just control documentation and incident reporting.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
